records.ety: [OE. listnen, listen, lustnen, lusten, AS. hlystan; akin to hlyst hearing, OS. hlust, Icel. hlusta to listen, hlust ear, AS. hlosnian to wait in suspense, OHG. hlosēn to listen, Gr. kly`ein, and E. loud. √41. See Loud, and cf. List to listen.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Listened (ls"'nd); p. pr. & vb. n. Listening.]

defs: To give close attention with the purpose of hearing; to give ear; to hearken; to attend.
quotes: When we have occasion to listen, and give a more particular attention to some sound, the tympanum is drawn to a more than ordinary tension.

defs: To give heed; to yield to advice; to follow admonition; to obey.
quotes: Listen to me, and by me be ruled.
Soldiers note forts, armories, and magazines; scholars listen after libraries, disputations, and professors.
